name: notion-fetch
description:
Retrieve the content of a Notion page, database, or data source by its ID or URL.
Provide a URL or ID in the `id` parameter. Make multiple calls to fetch multiple entities.

## Valid `id` formats (use ONLY these)

1. A full Notion page URL (must include the https:// domain):
   `https://www.notion.so/workspace/Page-Title-35c5913a695980d0868dc416007da734`
   `https://www.notion.so/35c5913a695980d0868dc416007da734`
2. A Notion Sites URL:
   `https://myspace.notion.site/Page-Title-abc123def456`
3. A raw UUID (with or without dashes) -- 32 hex characters only, no text prefix:
   `35c5913a-6959-80d0-868d-c416007da734`
   `35c5913a695980d0868dc416007da734`
4. A data source URL for database collections:
   `collection://12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef`

## INVALID `id` values -- these WILL return a validation_error

Do NOT pass any of the following as `id`:
- Page titles or names: "Verwendete Geraete", "Nihilistic Stoicism", "GaiaBuilder Notion Hub"
- URL slug fragments (title-hexchars without the domain): "Story-Timeline-35c5913a69598081b59cc7c87e4161a9", "Character-Bios-35c5913a695980d0868dc416007da734". These look like they contain a UUID but they are NOT valid -- you must pass either the full URL (https://www.notion.so/...) or just the UUID portion alone.
- Resource URIs: "notion://docs/enhanced-markdown-spec" -- the notion:// scheme is for MCP resources, not for this tool's id parameter.
- Markdown-formatted URLs: "<https://www.notion.so/page>" -- do not wrap URLs in angle brackets or markdown syntax. Pass the raw URL string.
- Made-up or guessed UUIDs that were not returned by a previous tool call.

## When you only have a page name

If the user asks about a page by name or topic (e.g. "show me my meeting notes"), do NOT pass the name to notion-fetch. Instead:
1. Call notion-search with the page name as the query.
2. From the search results, take the `id` field of the matching result.
3. Call notion-fetch with that `id`.

## Error recovery

If notion-fetch returns a validation_error, do NOT retry with the same id in a different format. Instead, call notion-search to find the correct page ID and then retry with the ID from the search results.

## Parameters

- `id` (required): The URL, UUID, or data source URL to fetch.
- `include_discussions`: Set to true to see discussion counts and inline discussion markers. The output will include a `<page-discussions>` summary with `discussion://` URLs usable with notion-get-comments.

## What this returns

- **Pages**: Content in enhanced Markdown format with Notion-specific extensions.
- **Databases**: All data sources (collections). Each data source has a unique ID shown in `<data-source url="collection://...">` tags. Pass a data source ID to this tool to get its schema and properties. Use data source IDs with notion-update-data-source and query tools. Database responses also include available templates for each data source, which can be used with notion-create-pages and notion-update-page.

<example description="Fetch by full URL">{"id": "https://www.notion.so/workspace/Page-a1b2c3d4e5f67890"}</example>
<example description="Fetch by UUID">{"id": "12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef"}</example>
<example description="Fetch Notion Sites page">{"id": "https://myspace.notion.site/Page-Title-abc123def456"}</example>
<example description="Fetch with discussions">{"id": "12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef", "include_discussions": true}</example>
<example description="Fetch database data source">{"id": "collection://12345678-90ab-cdef-1234-567890abcdef"}</example>

## Presenting results to the user

- Summarize the page content naturally based on the user's question.
- Use the page title and structure to organize the response.
- NEVER fabricate or invent content -- only use what the tool returns.
- If the tool returns an error, tell the user and suggest trying a search to find the correct page.

id.description: The ID or URL of the Notion page, database, or data source to fetch. Supports notion.so URLs, Notion Sites URLs (*.notion.site), raw UUIDs, and data source URLs (collection://...). Pass "self" to fetch the connected workspace and user identity.

include_transcript.description: Whether to include meeting note transcripts. Defaults to false. When true, full transcripts are included; when false, a placeholder with the meeting note URL is shown instead.

include_discussions.description: Whether to include discussion/comment indicators in the page output. When true, adds a <page-discussions> summary with discussion count, preview snippets, and discussion:// URLs. Use with the get_comments tool to retrieve full discussion content. Defaults to false.
